default.summary: Mixed breed dogs are generally robust and resilient pets with a highly diverse genetic background, reducing the likelihood of inheriting recessive breed-specific genetic disorders.
careTips: Schedule annual veterinary check-ups.
Provide dental hygiene care 2-3 times per week.
Maintain current vaccinations and parasite preventatives.
dietaryNeeds: Balanced, life-stage appropriate diet (puppy, adult, senior).
Incorporate Omega-3 fatty acids for joint and coat health.
default.exerciseLevel: Moderate, requiring 45-60 minutes of daily physical activity and positive mental stimulation.

labrador retriever.summary: Labradors are highly energetic, friendly, and intelligent family companions who excel in retrieving and tracking. They are highly food-motivated, which requires careful caloric monitoring.
careTips: Engage in joint-friendly exercises like swimming.
Screen regularly for ocular conditions.
Use slow feeder bowls to mitigate bloat risks.
dietaryNeeds: Strictly controlled calorie intake to prevent obesity.
Glucosamine and chondroitin for advanced joint maintenance.
labrador retriever.exerciseLevel: High intensity, requiring 60-90 minutes of vigorous daily exercise, training, and water games.

german shepherd.summary: German Shepherds are legendary working dogs with unmatched courage, loyalty, and trainability. They possess a thick double coat and require active structural management.
careTips: Regular mental stimulation is critical to prevent destructive behaviors.
Frequent brushing of their dense double coat to handle shedding.
Monitor gaits annually for early symptoms of Degenerative Myelopathy.
dietaryNeeds: Highly digestible proteins to alleviate sensitive gastrointestinal tracts.
Probiotics and prebiotics to support optimal digestive enzyme function.
german shepherd.exerciseLevel: Very high, demanding upwards of 90 minutes of daily structured workouts and agility tasks.

golden retriever.summary: Golden Retrievers are energetic, trusting, and gentle companions with a beautiful water-repellent dense coat. They thrive on human companionship and positive reinforcement.
careTips: Regular veterinary cancer screenings absolute recommendation post-age 6.
Frequent ear cleanings due to moisture trap from floppy ears.
Ensure thorough drying after baths or swimming to avoid hot spots.
dietaryNeeds: Controlled omega fatty acid ratios for skin barrier support.
L-carnitine and taurine integrations for cardiac health maintenance.
golden retriever.exerciseLevel: High, including 60 minutes of daily fetching, trailing, or swimming.

french bulldog.summary: French Bulldogs are affectionate, low-energy companions with a distinct brachycephalic facial structure that dictates specialized breathing and heat care.
careTips: Strictly avoid heat, hot environments, and over-exertion.
Clean and dry facial folds regularly with antimicrobial wipes.
Use a harness instead of a collar to protect fragile airways.
dietaryNeeds: Hypoallergenic ingredients to limit severe dermatological triggers.
Calorie-dense small kibble to regulate swallowing and avoid weight gain.
french bulldog.exerciseLevel: Light, consisting of 20-30 minutes of leisurely walking, strictly during cooler times of the day.

poodle.summary: Poodles are exceptionally intelligent, highly active, and carry a iconic hypoallergenic low-shedding curly coat requiring professional grooming.
careTips: Professional coat grooming and clipping every 4-6 weeks.
Ear canal checking and plucking to prevent chronic yeast infections.
Highly complex command-work to occupy supreme problem-solving brains.
dietaryNeeds: High-quality animal fats for energy and hypoallergenic skin elasticity.
Balanced calcium-to-phosphorus ratios to keep fine skeleton lines rigid.
poodle.exerciseLevel: Moderate to high, needing 60 minutes of retrievals, intelligence puzzles, and outdoor walks.

beagle.summary: Beagles are cheerful, merry scent hounds guided entirely by their sense of smell, possessing an inherent drive to track scents.
careTips: Secure yards completely, as their instinct is to follow tracks at all costs.
Keep foods out of reach to avoid garbage ingestion behaviors.
Check long floppy ears for moisture buildup weekly.
dietaryNeeds: Highly portion-controlled kibble; Beagles can eat continuously if permitted.
High-fiber dietary fillers to help satisfy huge appetites safely.
beagle.exerciseLevel: Moderate, requiring 45-60 minutes of nosework, sniffing walks, and exploration.

corgi.summary: Pembroke Welsh Corgis are alert, highly energetic herding dogs possessing long backs and short, robust legs, requiring careful skeletal load management.
careTips: Strictly minimize jumping off furniture, beds, or high stairs.
Combat double-coat shedding with twice-weekly slicker brush sessions.
Engage in brain/tactical herding games to burn off work ethic.
dietaryNeeds: Exceptional weight control; even minor excess weight strains long spinal segments.
High protein, moderate fat to maintain muscular, compact mass.
corgi.exerciseLevel: Moderate to high, needing 60 minutes of walks, fetch games, and structure obedience.

default.summary: Domestic mixed breeds are generally highly self-sufficient, structurally robust, and possess excellent genetic diversity, reducing breed-specific risks.
careTips: Provide multiple clean water sources to maximize hydration.
Keep fresh scratching surfaces to facilitate skin shedding of claws.
Implement environmental enrichment like vertical cat trees.
dietaryNeeds: Incorporate wet food to promote high moisture absorption.
Premium protein diets centered around real meat instead of fillers.
default.exerciseLevel: Moderate, involving simulated hunting games with laser pointers or wand toys for 15-20 minutes daily.

persian.summary: Persians are dignified, quiet, and docile cats characterized by their magnificent long matching coats and unique flat face, requiring extensive manual care.
careTips: Daily coat grooming is mandatory to prevent painful mats.
Wipe tear stains from eyes gently with safe damp cloth daily.
Perform routine veterinary renal ultrasound checks for PKD.
dietaryNeeds: Specially shaped kibble to accommodate flat jaws.
Highly digestible formulas to limit hairball formations.
persian.exerciseLevel: Low, preferring short, calm play sessions punctuated by long, serene resting periods.

maine coon.summary: Maine Coons are the gentle giants of the feline world, loving, massive, and carrying a magnificent water-resistant shaggy coat optimized for cold climates.
commonIssues: Hip Dysplasia (rare in most smaller felines)
careTips: Genetic screening for HCM gene mutations is highly recommended.
Brush long undercoats twice weekly to minimize matting.
Ensure sturdy, extra-large scratchers and climbing structures.
dietaryNeeds: Enriched with taurine for strong heart muscle health.
Balanced joint protection nutrients to support their heavy bone structure.
maine coon.exerciseLevel: Moderate to active, enjoying interactive puzzle toys, larger climbs, and sometimes fetch.

siamese.summary: Siamese are highly vocal, incredibly social, and extremely intelligent companions who demand near-constant human interaction and mental engagement.
careTips: Provide intricate puzzle toys to feed their highly intelligent minds.
Keep environment calm; Siamese are prone to anxiety-related self-grooming.
Provide routine weight tracking to prevent stress on fine bone structures.
dietaryNeeds: Extremely lean proteins to maintain their signature streamlined musculature.
Add antioxidant complexes to guard delicate respiratory structures.
siamese.exerciseLevel: High, demanding active engagement, interactive toys, climbing towers, and puzzle hunt systems.

default.summary: Pocket pets, birds, fish, and exotic animals carry unique species-specific metabolic and environmental needs critical for overall wellness.
careTips: Prioritize exact enclosure humidity, temperature, and lighting ranges.
Perform weight checks once per month to monitor nutritional intake.
Provide species-appropriate substrates and chewables.
dietaryNeeds: Highly specialized diets matching natural organic foraging patterns.
Vitamin and mineral dusting for reptiles or specialized seed/pellet feeds.
default.exerciseLevel: Varies by species, requiring safe physical and sensory enrichment.

rabbit.summary: Rabbits are gentle herbivorous companion animals with fragile skeletons and complex, sensitive digestive tracts requiring continuous fiber.
careTips: Unlimited high-quality fresh grass hay must be available daily.
Ensure a completely bunny-proofed safe area to run and explore.
Provide wooden chew toys continuously to wear down growing teeth.
dietaryNeeds: 80-90% fresh grass hay (like Timothy), supplemented by leafy green vegetables and small portions of pellets.
Strictly avoid sugary fruits, seeds, and commercial yogurt drops.
rabbit.exerciseLevel: Moderate to high, requiring 3-4 hours of daily free-roaming exercise in a fully secure area.
